generated_comment:
A: Bro, you really need to stop saying those sister-mother type gaalis in every sentence.
B: Arre yaar, itna deep mat le. Sab bolte hain, it’s just expression only, not like I’m actually talking about anyone’s mother or sister.
B: And when a guy starts crying over small pressure, what else will people say? Thoda strong ban, don’t behave like that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/chastity-rooted abuse as harmless speech and dismisses criticism with the 'just expression' defense. It also uses feminized behavior as an insult, implying that being like a woman is weak or shameful.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
